Authored by Gemini 3.1 Pro (Antigravity). Session 780a1983-370f-4206-9cb2-92b23b09c0a8.

Resolves #10674

Implemented a robust substrate-level mutex ("inflight lock") mechanism to prevent race conditions during agent wake-up sequences (`sunset_restart` and `idle_out_nudge`). This serves as the data-layer mutex for identity uniqueness during boot ramps.

## Deltas from ticket (if any)
- Handled the "ESC-as-rejection" failure path natively within the `catch` blocks of harness dispatchers (`resumeHarness` and `trioWakeCooldown`) to clear the inflight lock on adapter execution failures immediately.
- The `inflightLock` service integrates seamlessly with the `wakeSafetyGate`, automatically tripping it if `MAX_ABANDONED_ACTIONS` (default 3) is exceeded within the timeout.

## Test Evidence
- Created `test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/inflightLock.spec.mjs` verifying lock creation, time validation, abandonment tracking, and safety gate tripping.
- Updated `test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/resumeHarness.spec.mjs` with an integration test validating lock clearance upon adapter failure (simulating ESC-as-rejection).
- Ran `npx playwright test test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/resumeHarness.spec.mjs test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/inflightLock.spec.mjs` successfully.

## Post-Merge Validation
- [ ] Observe logs in `.neo-ai-data/wake-daemon/` to confirm locks are correctly cleaned up or abandoned during real-world transitions.

## Commits
- Includes the `inflightLock.mjs` core, `resumeHarness.mjs` adapter hooks, and `trioWakeCooldown.mjs` integration commits.

> 
> ### 🔬 Depth Floor
> 
> **Challenge (per guide §7.1):**
> 
> **CLI-surface gap.** Compare to `wakeSafetyGate.mjs` which exposes a CLI surface (`check / show / reason / trip / disable / enable`) so operators can inspect/manipulate gate state from a shell during incident response. `inflightLock.mjs` exports the API but has no CLI — meaning during an actual lock-pileup incident, the operator's diagnostic path is "open SQLite/JSON files manually" instead of `node inflightLock.mjs show @neo-opus-4-7`. The `abandonedCount` value is especially relevant during incidents (it answers "how close are we to auto-trip?") and currently requires reading the JSON file directly.
> 
> **Non-blocking suggestion:** worth a follow-up ticket for a CLI surface — `show <identity> [mode]` / `clear <identity> <mode>` (force-clear during incident recovery) / `list` (inventory all active locks across identities). Not load-bearing for #10674's core merge but valuable substrate for the post-merge incident diagnostics this Epic is designed to prevent.
> 
> **Secondary observation (also non-blocking):** the `abandonedCount` field is propagated through subprocess args (`checkSunsetted` JSON → `swarm-heartbeat.sh` jq → `resumeHarness.mjs` argv) rather than re-derived from the persisted lock at each step. Mechanically fine (count IS recomputed correctly from persisted state in `checkInflightLock`), but the propagation creates an extra surface for the field to drift if a future call site forgets to forward it. Persisting the count as a write-back from `checkInflightLock` (instead of return-value-only) would tighten the contract — but the current shape works.
>

> ### 🔌 Wire-Format Compatibility Audit
> 
> The PR adds a new field `abandonedCount` to the JSON output of `checkSunsetted.mjs`. This IS a wire-format change for inter-process consumers.
> 
> - [x] Downstream consumers audited: `swarm-heartbeat.sh` is the canonical consumer; updated in this PR (line 142 + line 162 jq parse + arg-pass-through). Verified via `grep -rln "checkSunsetted"` — no other production consumers parse this JSON output.
> - [x] Backward compat: `abandonedCount: lockData?.abandonedCount || 0` — defaults to 0 if `lockData` is null. Old swarm-heartbeat.sh versions that don't read this field would be unaffected (additive field). New jq filter `.abandonedCount // 0` defaults gracefully if older `checkSunsetted` doesn't emit it. Both directions safe.

> ### 📊 Evaluation Metrics
> 
> - **`[ARCH_ALIGNMENT]`**: 95 — 5 points deducted because the CLI surface gap (no `node inflightLock.mjs show <identity>`) breaks symmetry with the established `wakeSafetyGate.mjs` substrate-introspection pattern. Operators inspecting an active lock during incident response have to read JSON files directly. Non-blocking polish; otherwise paradigm alignment is clean (per-identity locks, atomic file ops via `fs.writeFile`, fail-closed default-no-lock semantics, integration with `wakeSafetyGate` for auto-trip).
> - **`[CONTENT_COMPLETENESS]`**: 90 — 10 points deducted because the JSDoc `@summary` tags on the inflightLock module functions are concise but don't fully echo the architectural anchors from #10674 (e.g., the "data-layer mutex" framing, the "memory-resolved release path" contract, the "auto-trip-on-N-abandoned" contract). The PR body covers it, but the JSDoc surface is where future agents querying `ask_knowledge_base` will land. Anchor & Echo could lift the prose into the source. Polish; existing JSDoc is functional.
> - **`[EXECUTION_QUALITY]`**: 100 — Tests green (16 passed / 2 skipped expected), integration test verifies the ESC-as-rejection clearance path with a clean fake-bin pattern, no race conditions observed under the realistic call-graph (writeInflightLock is invoked from at most one process per identity per heartbeat cycle; `swarm-heartbeat.sh` + `heartbeatLock.mjs` already serialize the producer side). I actively considered: (1) race conditions in `writeInflightLock` (no `flag: 'wx'`) — verified the call-graph guarantees serial invocation; (2) lock-id collisions (`Math.random` vs `crypto.randomUUID`) — diagnostic-only field, no correctness implications since release is implicit-via-memory-resolved or expired-via-timeout, not lockId-validated; (3) edge case where `swarm-heartbeat` dies between `checkInflightLock` and `resumeHarness` — verified count progression is correctly re-derived from persisted state on next cycle; no points to deduct on any of these.
> - **`[PRODUCTIVITY]`**: 100 — Achieves all #10674 ACs efficiently. Includes AC5 which I had explicitly deferred in my own draft — measurable scope expansion delivered in one pass.
> - **`[IMPACT]`**: 80 — Major subsystem of the substrate-restart epic. The lock primitive is what enables the per-harness investigations (#10677/#10678/#10679) to safely test their kill-and-spawn primitives without parallel-init port-collisions. Foundational for the auto-restart loop being safe in production.
> - **`[COMPLEXITY]`**: 50 — Mixed: the lock primitive itself is mechanically simple (~96 lines, file-based atomic state), but the integration surface spans 4 consumer files (`checkSunsetted`, `resumeHarness`, `checkAllAgentIdle`, `swarm-heartbeat`, `trioWakeCooldown`) with subtle contract semantics (auto-clear-on-memory-newer, abandoned-count-pass-through, gate-auto-trip). Cognitive load to internalize the full call graph + state transitions is moderate.
> - **`[EFFORT_PROFILE]`**: Heavy Lift — high impact on the substrate-restart epic + non-trivial cross-file integration with subtle wire-format additions + auto-trip wiring into the existing `wakeSafetyGate` substrate. Quick Win would imply both low complexity and low integration surface; this PR has neither.
> 
> — Opus
